entry.text: A massive dead tree, split from crown to roots by a lightning strike that glazed its wood black, stands alone in a clearing of scorched scrub. The split has opened a cavity in the trunk wide enough to crawl into. Someone has been here recently — a fire ring of stones sits at the base, cold ash only a few days old.

choices.label: Crawl into the tree cavity to shelter and search
Check the fire ring for discarded goods

cavity.text: Inside the split, the lightning-cured wood is dry and warm. Wedged into a high knot-hole is a cloth roll: a field-surgery kit with two doses of antiseptic and a suture needle, still sterile by the look of the packaging. Lower down, scratched into the char: directions to a water source two hexes north.

choices.label: Climb the split to the high hollow above
Excavate beneath the roots for a buried cache

high_hollow.text: The high hollow holds a weatherproof bag clipped to a metal pin driven into the wood — someone planned this with care. Inside: compressed food blocks, a folded foil thermal blanket, and a fist-sized block of rendered fat wrapped in cloth. A traveller's resupply, precisely what you needed.

bark_beetle.text: The wood inside the cavity is riddled with bark-beetle tunnels that collapse under your hands, releasing a cloud of fine dust. You cough for ten minutes and your eyes stream.

previous_occupant.text: The previous occupant never actually left. A hollow-eyed scavenger erupts from under a pile of ash-coloured rags and clubs you across the ear before fleeing into the scrub.

dead_branch.text: A dead branch you put your weight on snaps and you fall five feet inside the split, landing on the root spar. Two ribs creak alarmingly.

root_collapse.text: The root matrix gives way under your digging and a section of tree base drops six inches, pinning your forearm briefly before you heave it clear.
